word_meanings: yatra—at which; kāle—time; tu—but; anāvṛittim—no return; āvṛittim—return; cha—also; eva—certainly; yoginaḥ—the yogis; prayātāḥ—having passed away; yānti—attain; tam—that; kālam—time; vakṣhyāmi—I shall describe; bharata-ṛiṣhabha—O best of the Bharatas
translation_english: O best of the Bharatas, I shall now explain to you the different times at which, passing away from this world, the yogi does or does not come back.
deep_dive_text: The Two Paths of Departure. Krishna begins to explain the 'mystic' timing of death. This refers to the external conditions (light, moon cycles, solar cycles) that symbolically or literally align with the soul's internal state during the 'Great Exit'.

original_script:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम्।
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः।।8.24।।
transliteration:
agnir jyotir ahaḥ śhuklaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam
tatra prayātā gachchhanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥ
word_meanings: agniḥ—fire; jyotiḥ—light; ahaḥ—day; śhuklaḥ—the white fortnight of the moon; ṣhaṭ-māsāḥ—the six months; uttara-ayaṇam—when the sun passes on the northern side; tatra—there; prayātāḥ—those who pass away; gachchhanti—attain; brahma—the Absolute; brahma-vidaḥ—persons who know the Absolute; janāḥ—people
translation_english: Those who know the Supreme Brahman attain that Supreme by passing away from the world during the influence of the fiery god, in the light, at an auspicious moment of the day, during the fortnight of the waxing moon, or during the six months when the sun travels in the north.
deep_dive_text: The Path of Light ($Uttarāyaṇa$). This represents the path of liberation. The 'Light', 'Day', and 'Waxing Moon' all symbolize the increasing of knowledge and the direction toward the spiritual sun. Sages who die with their 'internal light' fully lit are said to travel this path of no return.

original_script:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्।
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते।।8.25।।
transliteration:
dhūmo rātris tathā kṛiṣhṇaḥ ṣhaṇ-māsā dakṣhiṇāyanam
tatra chāndramasaṁ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate
word_meanings: dhūmaḥ—smoke; rātriḥ—night; tathā—as well as; kṛiṣhṇaḥ—the dark fortnight of the moon; ṣhaṭ-māsāḥ—the six months; dakṣhiṇa-ayanam—when the sun passes on the southern side; tatra—there; chāndramasam—the moon; jyotiḥ—light; yogī—the yogi; prāpya—attaining; nivartate—comes back
translation_english: The mystic who passes away from this world during the smoke, the night, the fortnight of the waning moon, or the six months when the sun passes to the south reaches the moon planet but again comes back.
deep_dive_text: The Path of Return ($Dakṣhiṇāyanam$). In contrast to the path of light, this 'smoky' path represents those who perform good deeds for the sake of material or heavenly rewards. They attain 'lunar' (temporary) happiness, but because their desires are still anchored in the world, they must eventually 'Nivartate'—return to earth to take birth again.

original_script:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते।
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुनः।।8.26।।
transliteration:
śhukla-kṛiṣhṇe gatī hy ete jagataḥ śhāśhvate mate
ekayā yāty anāvṛittim anyayāvartate punaḥ
word_meanings: śhukla—light; kṛiṣhṇe—and darkness; gatī—ways of passing; hi—certainly; ete—these two; jagataḥ—of the world; śhāśhvate—eternal; mate—in the opinion; ekayā—by one; yāti—goes; anāvṛittim—to no return; anyayā—by the other; āvartate—comes back; punaḥ—again
translation_english: According to Vedic opinion, there are two ways of passing from this world—one in light and one in darkness. When one passes in light, he does not come back; but when one passes in darkness, he returns.
deep_dive_text: The Binary of Destiny. Krishna simplifies the cosmic complexity into two 'eternal' paths: Light ($Śhukla$) and Dark ($Kṛiṣhṇa$). This is not about the time on a clock, but the internal state of the soul—clarity versus cloudiness. One leads to the 'Dhāma' (Abode), the other back to the 'Duḥkhālayam' (World of Misery).

original_script:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन।
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन।।8.27।।
transliteration:
naite sṛitī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaśhchana
tasmāt sarveṣhu kāleṣhu yoga-yukto bhavārjuna
word_meanings: na—never; ete—these two; sṛitī—paths; pārtha—Arjun; jānan—knowing; yogī—the yogi; muhyati—is deluded; kaśhchana—anyone; tasmāt—therefore; sarveṣhu—at all; kāleṣhu—times; yoga-yuktaḥ—engaged in Yoga; bhava—become; arjuna—Arjun
translation_english: Although the yogis know these two paths, O Arjuna, they are never bewildered. Therefore be always fixed in devotion.
deep_dive_text: Immunity to Delusion. A person who understands the mechanics of life and death doesn't panic. They know that as long as they are 'Yoga-yuktaḥ' (connected to the Divine), the 'timing' or the 'path' doesn't matter, because the Divine will carry them home. Krishna’s final command here is: 'At all times, be a Yogi.'

original_script:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम्।
अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम्।।8.28।।
transliteration:
vedeṣhu yajñeṣhu tapaḥsu chaiva
dāneṣhu yat puṇya-phalaṁ pradiṣhṭam
atyeti tat sarvam idaṁ viditvā
yogī paraṁ sthānam upaiti chādyam
word_meanings: vedeṣhu—in the study of the Vedas; yajñeṣhu—in the performances of yajña; tapaḥsu—in various penances; cha—also; eva—certainly; dāneṣhu—in giving charities; yat—which; puṇya-phalam—result of pious work; pradiṣhṭam—directed; atyeti—surpasses; tat sarvam—all those; idam—this; viditvā—knowing; yogī—the yogi; param—supreme; sthānam—abode; upaiti—reaches; cha—also; ādyam—original
translation_english: A person who accepts the path of devotional service is not bereft of the results derived from studying the Vedas, performing sacrifices, giving in charity or pursuing philosophical and fruitive activities. Simply by performing devotional service, he attains all these, and at the end he reaches the supreme eternal abode.
deep_dive_text: The All-Inclusive Path. Krishna ends the chapter with a massive promise: Yoga is a shortcut that encompasses all other spiritual merits.

original_script: अथ नवमोऽध्यायः - राजविद्याराजगुह्ययोगः
transliteration: Rāja-Vidyā Rāja-Guhya Yogaḥ
word_meanings: Rāja—Sovereign; Vidyā—Knowledge; Guhya—Secret; Yogaḥ—the path of synthesis.
translation_english: Chapter 9 Summary: The Yoga of Sovereign Knowledge and the Sovereign Secret
deep_dive_text: Chapter 9 represents the historical consolidation of the 'Bhakti' (devotional) movement into the Vedic framework. It reveals the 'Sovereign Knowledge'—the panentheistic nature of the Divine as both the origin and the pervading essence of the universe. It explicitly critiques the 'transactional' nature of Vedic rituals (seeking heaven/Svarga) and proposes a direct, non-intermediated relationship with the Supreme.

original_script:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्।।9.1।।
transliteration:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
idaṁ tu te guhyatamaṁ pravakṣhyāmyanasūyave
jñānaṁ vijñāna-sahitaṁ yajjñātvā mokṣhyase ’śhubhāt
word_meanings: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said; idam—this; tu—but; te—to you; guhya-tamam—the most confidential; pravakṣhyāmi—I shall explain; anasūyave—to the non-envious; jñānam—knowledge; vijñāna—realized knowledge; sahitam—with; yat—which; jñātvā—knowing; mokṣhyase—you will be freed; aśhubhāt—from the miserable material existence
translation_english: The Blessed Lord said: My dear Arjuna, because you are never envious of Me, I shall impart to you this most confidential knowledge and realization, knowing which you shall be relieved of the miseries of material existence.
deep_dive_text: The Secret of Non-Envy. Krishna begins the chapter by stating the primary qualification for receiving profound truth: 'Anasuyave' (non-envious). If a student is secretly competing with the teacher or envious of the truth, the mind acts like a closed fist. True wisdom only enters a relaxed, receptive, and trusting heart.

original_script:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम्।
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम्।।9.2।।
transliteration:
rāja-vidyā rāja-guhyaṁ pavitram idam uttamam
pratyakṣhāvagamaṁ dharmyaṁ su-sukhaṁ kartum avyayam
word_meanings: rāja-vidyā—the king of education; rāja-guhyam—the king of secrets; pavitram—pure; idam—this; uttamam—highest; pratyakṣha—direct; avagamam—perception; dharmyam—the principle of religion; su-sukham—very joyful; kartum—to execute; avyayam—everlasting
translation_english: This knowledge is the king of education, the most secret of all secrets. It is the purest knowledge, and because it gives direct perception of the self by realization, it is the perfection of religion. It is everlasting, and it is joyfully performed.
deep_dive_text: The King of Secrets. Krishna brands this wisdom as 'Raja-vidya' (the sovereign science). Unlike difficult, esoteric ascetic practices, this path is 'Su-sukham' (joyful to perform) and 'Avyayam' (everlasting). The greatest truths aren't necessarily the hardest to practice; they are simply the most direct.

original_script:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप।
अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि।।9.3।।
transliteration:
aśhraddadhānāḥ puruṣhā dharmasyāsya parantapa
aprāpya māṁ nivartante mṛityu-saṁsāra-vartmani
word_meanings: aśhraddadhānāḥ—those who are faithless; puruṣhāḥ—persons; dharmasya—of this religion; asya—this; parantapa—O conqueror of enemies; aprāpya—without obtaining; mām—Me; nivartante—they come back; mṛityu—of death; saṁsāra—in material existence; vartmani—on the path
translation_english: Those who are not faithful in this devotional service cannot attain Me, O conqueror of enemies. Therefore they return to the path of birth and death in this material world.
deep_dive_text: The Faith Requirement. 'Shraddha' (faith) isn't blind belief; it's the working hypothesis required to run the experiment of spirituality. Without it, the seeker gives up at the first sign of difficulty, falling back into the default cycle of 'Samsara' (the repetitive loop of temporary gains and inevitable losses).

original_script:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना।
मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः।।9.4।।
transliteration:
mayā tatam idaṁ sarvaṁ jagad avyakta-mūrtinā
mat-sthāni sarva-bhūtāni na chāhaṁ teṣhvavasthitaḥ
word_meanings: mayā—by Me; tatam—pervaded; idam—this; sarvam—all; jagat—cosmic manifestation; avyakta-mūrtinā—by the unmanifested form; mat-sthāni—in Me; sarva-bhūtāni—all living entities; na—not; cha—also; aham—I; teṣhu—in them; avasthitaḥ—situated
translation_english: By Me, in My unmanifested form, this entire universe is pervaded. All beings are in Me, but I am not in them.
deep_dive_text: The Quantum Paradox of the Divine. Krishna presents a mind-bending concept: He is everywhere, holding everything together, yet He is not 'contained' by anything. Like a Wi-Fi signal in a room—it enables all the devices to connect and function, but the signal isn't trapped inside the hardware.

original_script:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्।
भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः।।9.5।।
transliteration:
na cha mat-sthāni bhūtāni paśhya me yogam aiśhvaram
bhūta-bhṛin na cha bhūta-stho mamātmā bhūta-bhāvanaḥ
word_meanings: na—never; cha—also; mat-sthāni—situated in Me; bhūtāni—all creation; paśhya—see; me—My; yogam aiśhvaram—inconceivable mystic power; bhūta-bhṛit—the maintainer of all living entities; na—never; cha—also; bhūta-sthaḥ—in the cosmic manifestation; mama—My; ātmā—Self; bhūta-bhāvanaḥ—the source of all manifestations
translation_english: And yet everything that is created does not rest in Me. Behold My mystic opulence! Although I am the maintainer of all living entities and although I am everywhere, I am not a part of this cosmic manifestation, for My Self is the very source of creation.
deep_dive_text: The Divine Magic Trick. Right after saying 'all beings are in Me,' Krishna says, 'they are not in Me!' This 'Aishvaram Yoga' (divine mysticism) breaks human logic. It means that while reality depends entirely on the Divine, the Divine is completely independent of reality. God is the author, not the book.

original_script:
यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान्।
तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय।।9.6।।
transliteration:
yathākāśha-sthito nityaṁ vāyuḥ sarvatra-go mahān
tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni mat-sthānītyupadhāraya
word_meanings: yathā—just as; ākāśha-sthitaḥ—situated in the sky; nityam—always; vāyuḥ—the wind; sarvatra-gaḥ—blowing everywhere; mahān—great; tathā—similarly; sarvāṇi bhūtāni—all created beings; mat-sthāni—situated in Me; iti—thus; upadhāraya—try to understand
translation_english: Understand that as the mighty wind, blowing everywhere, rests always in the sky, all created beings rest in Me.
deep_dive_text: The Sky and the Wind. A beautiful analogy to clarify the paradox of the previous verse. The wind is powerful, chaotic, and moves everywhere, but it always exists within the vast, silent container of the sky. The sky is unaffected by the storms. Similarly, the entire drama of the universe plays out within the infinite container of Divine consciousness.

original_script:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम्।
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम्।।9.7।।
transliteration:
sarva-bhūtāni kaunteya prakṛitiṁ yānti māmikām
kalpa-kṣhaye punas tāni kalpādau visṛijāmyaham
word_meanings: sarva-bhūtāni—all created beings; kaunteya—O son of Kunti; prakṛitim—material nature; yānti—enter; māmikām—My; kalpa-kṣhaye—at the end of the millennium; punaḥ—again; tāni—all those; kalpa-ādau—in the beginning of the millennium; visṛijāmi—create; aham—I
translation_english: O son of Kunti, at the end of the millennium all material manifestations enter into My nature, and at the beginning of another millennium, by My potency, I create them again.
deep_dive_text: The Cosmic Breath. Time is not a straight line; it is a circle. Krishna describes the 'Kalpa' (cosmic cycle). Just as we exhale and inhale, the universe expands into manifestation and contracts back into the unmanifested state. The entirety of cosmic history is just the rhythmic breathing of the Divine.

original_script:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः।
भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात्।।9.8।।
transliteration:
prakṛitiṁ svām avaṣhṭabhya visṛijāmi punaḥ punaḥ
bhūta-grāmam imaṁ kṛitsnam avaśhaṁ prakṛitervaśhāt
word_meanings: prakṛitim—the material nature; svām—My own; avaṣhṭabhya—entering into; visṛijāmi—I create; punaḥ punaḥ—again and again; bhūta-grāmam—all the cosmic manifestations; imam—this; kṛitsnam—in total; avaśham—automatically; prakṛiteḥ—of the force of nature; vaśhāt—under obligation
translation_english: The whole cosmic order is under Me. Under My will it is automatically manifested again and again, and under My will it is annihilated at the end.
deep_dive_text: The Automated Universe. Krishna explains that nature (Prakriti) does the heavy lifting. Beings are 'Avasham' (helpless) under the laws of nature—compelled to act, be born, and die according to their conditioning. The Divine acts as the underlying 'Will' or software code that runs this massive automated simulation.

original_script:
न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय।
उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु।।9.9।।
transliteration:
na cha māṁ tāni karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
udāsīnavad āsīnam asaktaṁ teṣhu karmasu
word_meanings: na—never; cha—also; mām—Me; tāni—all those; karmāṇi—activities; nibadhnanti—bind; dhanañjaya—O conqueror of wealth; udāsīna-vat—as neutral; āsīnam—situated; asaktam—without attachment; teṣhu—to those; karmasu—in activities
translation_english: O Dhananjaya, all this work cannot bind Me. I am ever detached from all these material activities, seated as though neutral.
deep_dive_text: The Art of Neutrality. How do you manage a whole universe without getting stressed? 'Udasina-vat'—acting like a neutral observer. Krishna participates in creation but remains 'Asaktam' (unattached). This is the ultimate lesson for the user: You can be deeply involved in your work while remaining internally unbothered by the chaos.

original_script:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम्।
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते।।9.10।।
transliteration:
mayādhyakṣheṇa prakṛitiḥ sūyate sa-charācharam
hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate
word_meanings: mayā—by Me; adhyakṣheṇa—by superintendence; prakṛitiḥ—material nature; sūyate—manifests; sa—with; chara-acharam—the moving and the nonmoving; hetunā—for the reason; anena—this; kaunteya—O son of Kunti; jagat—the cosmic manifestation; viparivartate—is working
translation_english: This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kunti, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.
deep_dive_text: The Silent Supervisor. Krishna is the 'Adhyaksha' (overseer or director). He doesn't micromanage the universe; He provides the conscious presence that allows Nature (Prakriti) to function. It’s like the sun: the sun doesn't actively grow the plants, but without its presence, nothing grows. The Divine Presence is the ultimate catalyst.

original_script:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम्।
परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम्।।9.11।।
transliteration:
avajānanti māṁ mūḍhā mānuṣhīṁ tanum āśhritam
paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūta-maheśhvaram
word_meanings: avajānanti—deride; mām—Me; mūḍhāḥ—fools; mānuṣhīm—in a human; tanum—form; āśhritam—having assumed; param—supreme; bhāvam—nature; ajānantaḥ—not knowing; mama—My; bhūta—of everything that be; mahā-īśhvaram—the supreme Lord
translation_english: Fools deride Me when I descend in the human form. They do not know My transcendental nature as the Supreme Lord of all that be.
deep_dive_text: The Illusion of the Ordinary. Because Krishna appears as a regular human—eating, talking, and driving a chariot—those with superficial vision ('mudhas' or fools) mistake Him for just another powerful king or historical figure. They miss the 'Param Bhavam' (Supreme Nature) hidden behind the human disguise. This warns against judging the depth of the Divine by external appearances.

original_script:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः।
राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः।।9.12।।
transliteration:
moghāśhā mogha-karmāṇo mogha-jñānā vichetasaḥ
rākṣhasīm āsurīṁ chaiva prakṛitiṁ mohinīṁ śhritāḥ
word_meanings: mogha-āśhāḥ—baffled in their hopes; mogha-karmāṇaḥ—baffled in their fruitive activities; mogha-jñānāḥ—baffled in their knowledge; vichetasaḥ—bewildered; rākṣhasīm—demoniac; āsurīm—atheistic; cha—and; eva—certainly; prakṛitim—nature; mohinīm—deluding; śhritāḥ—taking shelter of
translation_english: Those who are thus bewildered are attracted by demonic and atheistic views. In that deluded condition, their hopes for liberation, their fruitive activities, and their culture of knowledge are all defeated.
deep_dive_text: The Trap of Cynicism. When someone loses faith in the Divine and adopts an 'Asuric' (demonic/exploitative) or purely materialistic worldview, their entire life trajectory collapses. Krishna uses the word 'Mogha' (baffled/useless) three times: their hopes, their actions, and their knowledge ultimately lead nowhere because they are disconnected from the source of reality.

original_script:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः।
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम्।।9.13।।
transliteration:
mahātmānas tu māṁ pārtha daivīṁ prakṛitim āśhritāḥ
bhajantyananya-manaso jñātvā bhūtādim avyayam
word_meanings: mahā-ātmānaḥ—the great souls; tu—but; mām—unto Me; pārtha—O son of Pritha; daivīm—divine; prakṛitim—nature; āśhritāḥ—having taken shelter of; bhajanti—engage in devotion; ananya-manasaḥ—with mind fixed exclusively; jñātvā—knowing; bhūta-ādim—the origin of creation; avyayam—imperishable
translation_english: O son of Pritha, those who are not deluded, the great souls, are under the protection of the divine nature. They are fully engaged in devotional service because they know Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, original and inexhaustible.
deep_dive_text: The Mark of a Mahatma. In contrast to the fools, the 'Mahatmas' (Great Souls) take shelter of the Divine Nature. Their defining characteristic is an 'Ananya-manas'—an undivided mind. Because they have realized Krishna as the imperishable source of all existence, their devotion is not a chore, but an effortless, continuous flow.

original_script:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः।
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।।9.14।।
transliteration:
satataṁ kīrtayanto māṁ yatantaśh cha dṛiḍha-vratāḥ
namasyantaśh cha māṁ bhaktyā nitya-yuktā upāsate
word_meanings: satatam—always; kīrtayantaḥ—glorifying; mām—Me; yatantaḥ—striving; cha—also; dṛiḍha-vratāḥ—with firm determination; namasyantaḥ—bowing down; cha—also; mām—Me; bhaktyā—in devotion; nitya-yuktāḥ—constantly engaged; upāsate—worship
translation_english: Always chanting My glories, endeavoring with great determination, bowing down before Me, these great souls perpetually worship Me with devotion.
deep_dive_text: The Anatomy of Devotion. How does a Mahatma live? They are always engaged. 'Kirtayanto' means they celebrate and talk about the Divine. They have 'Dridha-vrata'—iron-clad determination in their spiritual practices. It’s a dynamic, active love, where every thought, word, and physical action is a bow of reverence to the Supreme.

original_script:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते।
एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम्।।9.15।।
transliteration:
jñāna-yajñena chāpyanye yajanto mām upāsate
ekatvena pṛithaktvena bahudhā viśhvato-mukham
word_meanings: jñāna-yajñena—by cultivation of knowledge; cha—also; api—certainly; anye—others; yajantaḥ—worshipping; mām—Me; upāsate—worship; ekatvena—in oneness; pṛithaktvena—in diversity; bahudhā—in various ways; viśhvataḥ-mukham—in the universal form
translation_english: Others, who engage in sacrifice by the cultivation of knowledge, worship the Supreme Lord as the one without a second, as diverse in many, and in the universal form.
deep_dive_text: The Infinite Faces of Worship. Krishna acknowledges that not everyone is a traditional 'devotee'. Some worship through 'Jnana-Yajna' (the sacrifice of intellect/knowledge). They might see God as the formless 'One', or see the Divine in the diverse elements of nature, or meditate on the terrifying, all-encompassing Universal Form. All are valid paths.

original_script:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम्।
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम्।।9.16।।
transliteration:
ahaṁ kratur ahaṁ yajñaḥ svadhāham aham auṣhadham
mantro ’ham aham evājyam aham agnir ahaṁ hutam
word_meanings: aham—I; kratuḥ—Vedic ritual; aham—I; yajñaḥ—sacrifice; svadhā—offering to the ancestors; aham—I; aham—I; auṣhadham—healing herb/medicine; mantraḥ—transcendental chant; aham—I; aham—I; eva—certainly; ājyam—clarified butter; aham—I; agniḥ—fire; aham—I; hutam—the offering
translation_english: But it is I who am the ritual, I the sacrifice, the offering to the ancestors, the healing herb, the transcendental chant. I am the butter and the fire and the offering.
deep_dive_text: The All-Encompassing Reality. In a Vedic ritual, there are many components: the fire, the butter, the mantra, the person doing it. Krishna collapses all these into Himself. He is saying, 'Don't get lost in the mechanics of the ritual; I am every single piece of it.' The means and the end are both Divine.

original_script:
पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः।
वेद्यं पवित्रमोङ्कार ऋक्साम यजुरेव च।।9.17।।
transliteration:
pitāham asya jagato mātā dhātā pitāmahaḥ
vedyaṁ pavitram oṅkāra ṛik sāma yajur eva cha
word_meanings: pitā—father; aham—I; asya—of this; jagataḥ—universe; mātā—mother; dhātā—supporter; pitāmahaḥ—grandfather; vedyam—what is to be known; pavitram—that which purifies; oṅkāraḥ—the syllable Om; ṛik—the Rig Veda; sāma—the Sama Veda; yajuḥ—the Yajur Veda; eva—certainly; cha—and
translation_english: I am the father of this universe, the mother, the support and the grandsire. I am the object of knowledge, the purifier and the syllable om. I am also the Rig, the Sama and the Yajur Vedas.
deep_dive_text: The Cosmic Family. Krishna personalizes the universe. He is the ultimate parent—both Mother and Father—providing unconditional love and structural support. He is also the root of all sacred knowledge, represented by the primal sound 'Om' and the foundational texts of human civilization.

original_script:
गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत्।
प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम्।।9.18।।
transliteration:
gatir bhartā prabhuḥ sākṣhī nivāsaḥ śharaṇaṁ suhṛit
prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ sthānaṁ nidhānaṁ bījam avyayam
word_meanings: gatiḥ—the goal; bhartā—the sustainer; prabhuḥ—the Lord; sākṣhī—the witness; nivāsaḥ—the abode; śharaṇam—the refuge; su-hṛit—the most intimate friend; prabhavaḥ—the creation; pralayaḥ—the dissolution; sthānam—the resting place; nidhānam—the resting place/treasure; bījam—the seed; avyayam—imperishable
translation_english: I am the goal, the sustainer, the master, the witness, the abode, the refuge, and the most dear friend. I am the creation and the annihilation, the basis of everything, the resting place and the eternal seed.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Safety Net. This verse offers total psychological security. Whatever you are looking for, Krishna is it. If you need a goal, He is 'Gati'. If you are lonely, He is 'Suhrit' (a well-wishing friend). If you need protection, He is 'Sharanam' (refuge). It is a poetic crescendo of divine omnipresence.

original_script:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च।
अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन।।9.19।।
transliteration:
tapāmy aham ahaṁ varṣhaṁ nigṛihṇāmy utsṛijāmi cha
amṛitaṁ chaiva mṛityuśh cha sad asach chāham arjuna
word_meanings: tapāmi—radiate heat; aham—I; aham—I; varṣham—rain; nigṛihṇāmi—withhold; utsṛijāmi—send forth; cha—and; amṛitam—immortality; cha—and; eva—certainly; mṛityuḥ—death; cha—and; sat—being/truth; asat—non-being/matter; cha—and; aham—I; arjuna—Arjun
translation_english: O Arjuna, I give heat, and I withhold and send forth the rain. I am immortality, and I am also death personified. Both spirit and matter are in Me.
deep_dive_text: Embracing the Paradox. The mind likes to categorize God as only 'good' or 'pleasant'. But Krishna claims everything: He is the life-giving rain, but also the scorching drought. He is immortality, but He is also the reality of Death. True spirituality requires accepting that both 'Sat' (existence) and 'Asat' (non-existence) flow from the same ultimate source.

original_script:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा
यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते।
ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक-
मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान्।।9.20।।
transliteration:
trai-vidyā māṁ soma-pāḥ pūta-pāpā
yajñair iṣhṭvā swar-gatiṁ prārthayante
te puṇyam āsādya surendra-lokam
aśhnanti divyān divi deva-bhogān
word_meanings: trai-vidyāḥ—the knowers of the three Vedas; mām—Me; soma-pāḥ—drinkers of the soma juice; pūta—purified; pāpāḥ—of sins; yajñaiḥ—by sacrifices; iṣhṭvā—worshipping; swaḥ-gatim—passage to heaven; prārthayante—pray for; te—they; puṇyam—pious; āsādya—attaining; sura-indra—of Indra; lokam—the world; aśhnanti—enjoy; divyān—celestial; divi—in heaven; deva-bhogān—the pleasures of the gods
translation_english: Those who study the Vedas and drink the soma juice, seeking the heavenly planets, worship Me indirectly. Purified of sinful reactions, they take birth on the pious, heavenly planet of Indra, where they enjoy godly delights.
deep_dive_text: The limitation of 'Transactional Religion'. Krishna points out that many people use religion (studying Vedas, doing rituals) just to get a 'ticket to heaven'. They get what they ask for—a vacation in the celestial realms with amazing pleasures. However, this is not liberation; it is merely an extended, luxurious stay in the material universe, from which they must eventually return.

original_script:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं
क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति।
एवं त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना
गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते।।9.21।।
transliteration:
te taṁ bhuktvā swarga-lokaṁ viśhālaṁ
kṣhīṇe puṇye martya-lokaṁ viśhanti
evaṁ trayī-dharmam anuprapannā
gatāgataṁ kāma-kāmā labhante
word_meanings: te—they; tam—that; bhuktvā—having enjoyed; swarga-lokam—the vast heavenly world; viśhālam—vast; kṣhīṇe—upon exhaustion; puṇye—of pious merit; martya-lokam—to the mortal world; viśhanti—they return; evam—thus; trayī-dharmam—the fruitive activities of the three Vedas; anuprapannāḥ—following; gata-āgatam—repeated coming and going; kāma-kāmāḥ—desiring sense enjoyments; labhante—attain
translation_english: When they have thus enjoyed vast heavenly sense pleasure and the results of their pious activities are exhausted, they return to this mortal planet again. Thus those who seek sense fruition by adhering to the principles of the three Vedas achieve only repeated birth and death.
deep_dive_text: The Cosmic Treadmill. Heavenly planets are not a permanent residence; they are like a high-end hotel. You can stay as long as you have the currency (Punya/merit), but once your balance is zero, you are sent back to the Earth (Martya-loka). Doing good merely for rewards keeps you trapped in the 'Gatagatam'—the endless cycle of coming and going.

original_script:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्।।9.22।।
transliteration:
ananyāśh chintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate
teṣhāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣhemaṁ vahāmy aham
word_meanings: ananyāḥ—without other object; chintayantaḥ—contemplating; mām—Me; ye—who; janāḥ—persons; paryupāsate—worship; teṣhām—of them; nitya-abhiyuktānām—who are constantly aligned; yoga—attainment of the unachieved; kṣhemam—preservation of the achieved; vahāmi—I carry; aham—I
translation_english: For those who contemplate Me with exclusive focus and are constantly aligned, I personally secure their requirements and preserve their acquisitions.
deep_dive_text: The terms 'Yoga' and 'Kshema' were originally legal and administrative terms in ancient India referring to the acquisition and protection of property. Krishna uses this terminology to promise that for the 'Ananya' (the focused seeker), the Divine assumes the administrative burden of their existence.

original_script:
येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः।
तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम्।।9.23।।
transliteration:
ye ’py anya-devatā bhaktā yajante śhraddhayānvitāḥ
te ’pi mām eva kaunteya yajanty avidhi-pūrvakam
word_meanings: ye—who; api—even; anya—other; devatā—gods; bhaktāḥ—devotees; yajante—worship; śhraddhayā anvitāḥ—with faith; te—they; api—also; mām—Me; eva—only; kaunteya—O son of Kunti; yajanti—worship; avidhi-pūrvakam—without the proper rules/indirectly
translation_english: Those who are devotees of other gods and who worship them with faith actually worship only Me, O son of Kunti, but they do so in a wrong way.
deep_dive_text: All Rivers Lead to the Ocean. Krishna acknowledges the diverse paths of worship. Even those who worship lesser deities or materialistic forces are ultimately tapping into His energy, because He is the source of all. However, doing so 'Avidhi-purvakam' (indirectly or without proper understanding) delays true liberation, keeping the worshipper stuck in lesser realities.

original_script:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च।
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते।।9.24।।
transliteration:
ahaṁ hi sarva-yajñānāṁ bhoktā cha prabhur eva cha
na tu mām abhijānanti tattvenātaśh chyavanti te
word_meanings: aham—I; hi—certainly; sarva—of all; yajñānām—sacrifices; bhoktā—the enjoyer; cha—and; prabhuḥ—the Lord; eva—also; cha—and; na—not; tu—but; mām—Me; abhijānanti—know; tattvena—in reality; ataḥ—therefore; chyavanti—fall down; te—they
translation_english: I am the only enjoyer and master of all sacrifices. Therefore, those who do not recognize My true transcendental nature fall down.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Recipient. All sacrifices, gratitude, and offerings are ultimately received by the Supreme. Ignorance of this fact means people aim their efforts at temporary goals or lesser entities. Because they miss the 'Tattva' (true essence), they 'fall down'—meaning they remain bound to the cycle of birth and death instead of graduating to eternity.

original_script:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान्पितॄन्यान्ति पितृव्रताः।
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम्।।9.25।।
transliteration:
yānti deva-vratā devān pitṝīn yānti pitṛi-vratāḥ
bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti mad-yājino ’pi mām
word_meanings: yānti—go; deva-vratāḥ—worshipers of demigods; devān—to the demigods; pitṝīn—to the ancestors; yānti—go; pitṛi-vratāḥ—worshipers of ancestors; bhūtāni—to the ghosts/spirits; yānti—go; bhūta-ijyāḥ—worshipers of ghosts; yānti—go; mat—My; yājinaḥ—devotees; api—but; mām—unto Me
translation_english: Those who worship the demigods will take birth among the demigods; those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors; those who worship ghosts and spirits will take birth among such beings; and those who worship Me will live with Me.
deep_dive_text: The Law of Spiritual Attraction. You get exactly what you aim for. Your destination is determined by your object of worship. If you invest your energy in temporary, material goals, you reap temporary results. But if your target is the Eternal, you achieve eternity. It’s a perfect equation of cause and effect.

original_script:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः।।9.26।।
transliteration:
patraṁ puṣhpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayachchhati
tad ahaṁ bhakty-upahṛitam aśhnāmi prayatātmanaḥ
word_meanings: patram—a leaf; puṣhpam—a flower; phalam—a fruit; toyam—water; yaḥ—whoever; me—unto Me; bhaktyā—with devotion; prayachchhati—offers; tat—that; aham—I; bhakti-upahṛitam—offered in devotion; aśhnāmi—accept; prayata-ātmanaḥ—from one in pure consciousness
translation_english: If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water, I will accept it.
deep_dive_text: The Accessibility of the Divine. You don’t need wealth, grand rituals, or complex ceremonies to reach God. The Supreme is satisfied with the simplest things—a leaf, a flower, or just water—provided they are offered with genuine love ('Bhakti'). It is the intention, not the item, that the Divine consumes.

original_script:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्।
यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम्।।9.27।।
transliteration:
yat karoṣhi yad aśhnāsi yaj juhoṣhi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣhva mad-arpaṇam
word_meanings: yat—whatever; karoṣhi—you do; yat—whatever; aśhnāsi—you eat; yat—whatever; juhoṣhi—you offer in sacrifice; dadāsi—you give away in charity; yat—whatever; yat—whatever; tapasyasi—austerities you perform; kaunteya—O son of Kunti; tat—that; kuruṣhva—do; mat—unto Me; arpaṇam—as an offering
translation_english: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform—do that, O son of Kunti, as an offering to Me.
deep_dive_text: Living as an Offering. Krishna turns all of life into a spiritual practice. You don't have to separate your 'spiritual life' from your 'regular life.' Eating, working, giving, and striving can all be transformed into powerful meditation simply by changing the intention. Do it all for the Divine.

original_script:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः।
संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि।।9.28।।
transliteration:
śhubhāśhubha-phalair evaṁ mokṣhyase karma-bandhanaiḥ
sannyāsa-yoga-yuktātmā vimukto mām upaiṣhyasi
word_meanings: śhubha—from good; aśhubha—and evil; phalaiḥ—results; evam—thus; mokṣhyase—you will be freed; karma—of work; bandhanaiḥ—from the bondage; sannyāsa—of renunciation; yoga—the yoga; yukta-ātmā—having the mind firmly set on; vimuktaḥ—liberated; mām—to Me; upaiṣhyasi—you will attain
translation_english: In this way you will be freed from bondage to work and its auspicious and inauspicious results. With your mind fixed on Me in this principle of renunciation, you will be liberated and come to Me.
deep_dive_text: True Renunciation. Renunciation (Sannyāsa) isn't about leaving your family or your job. It's about giving up the psychological ownership of the results. When everything is offered to the Divine, you stop accumulating 'Karmic baggage'—both good and bad—and experience complete inner freedom while still engaging with the world.

original_script:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम्।।9.29।।
transliteration:
samo ’haṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu na me dveṣhyo ’sti na priyaḥ
ye bhajanti tu māṁ bhaktyā mayi te teṣhu chāpy aham
word_meanings: samaḥ—equally disposed; aham—I; sarva-bhūteṣhu—to all living beings; na—no one; me—to Me; dveṣhyaḥ—hateful; asti—is; na—nor; priyaḥ—dear; ye—those who; bhajanti—render transcendental service; tu—but; mām—unto Me; bhaktyā—in devotion; mayi—are in Me; te—they; teṣhu—in them; cha—also; api—certainly; aham—I
translation_english: I envy no one, nor am I partial to anyone. I am equal to all. But whoever renders service unto Me in devotion is a friend, is in Me, and I am also a friend to him.
deep_dive_text: The Divine Neutrality and Intimacy. God is like the sun—shining equally on the saint and the sinner. The Divine doesn't have a 'favorites list'. However, if you step out of the shadows and into the sunlight (through Devotion/Bhakti), you naturally feel the warmth. The relationship becomes deeply reciprocal and intimate.

original_script:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक्।
साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः।।9.30।।
transliteration:
api chet su-durāchāro bhajate mām ananya-bhāk
sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ
word_meanings: api—even; chet—if; su-durāchāraḥ—one committing the most abominable actions; bhajate—is engaged in devotional service; mām—unto Me; ananya-bhāk—without deviation; sādhuḥ—a saint; eva—certainly; saḥ—he; mantavyaḥ—is to be considered; samyak—completely; vyavasitaḥ—situated in determination; hi—certainly; saḥ—he
translation_english: Even if one commits the most abominable action, if he is engaged in devotional service he is to be considered saintly because he is properly situated in his determination.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Redemption. This is a radical statement of grace. No matter how fractured your past is (su-durāchāro), if you pivot and sincerely direct your mind toward the Divine without deviation, you are instantly redefined. You are considered a 'Sadhu' (saint) not because you are flawless, but because your trajectory is now perfectly aimed.

original_script:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति।
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति।।9.31।।
transliteration:
kṣhipraṁ bhavati dharmātmā śhaśhvach-chhāntiṁ nigachchhati
kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśhyati
word_meanings: kṣhipram—very soon; bhavati—becomes; dharma-ātmā—righteous; śhaśhvat-śhāntim—lasting peace; nigachchhati—attains; kaunteya—O son of Kunti; pratijānīhi—declare; na—never; me—My; bhaktaḥ—devotee; praṇaśhyati—perishes
translation_english: He quickly becomes righteous and attains lasting peace. O son of Kunti, declare it boldly that My devotee never perishes.
deep_dive_text: The Transformation. Because the trajectory is right, the transformation is 'Kshipram' (immediate or very soon). The bad habits fall away because the heart has found a higher taste. Krishna is so confident in this process of grace that He asks Arjuna to be the one to declare it: 'Na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśhyati'—My devotee is never destroyed. The spiritual bank balance is never lost.

original_script:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः।
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम्।।9.32।।
transliteration:
māṁ hi pārtha vyapāśhritya ye ’pi syuḥ pāpa-yonayaḥ
striyo vaiśhyās tathā śhūdrās te ’pi yānti parāṁ gatim
word_meanings: mām—of Me; hi—certainly; pārtha—O son of Pritha; vyapāśhritya—taking shelter; ye—who; api—also; syuḥ—are; pāpa-yonayaḥ—born of lower backgrounds; striyaḥ—women; vaiśhyāḥ—merchants; tathā—also; śhūdrāḥ—workers; te api—even they; yānti—go; parām—to the supreme; gatim—destination
translation_english: O son of Pritha, those who take shelter in Me, though they be of lower birth—women, vaishyas (merchants) and shudras (workers)—can attain the supreme destination.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Inclusivity. This verse shatters the rigid social hierarchies of ancient times. Spiritual liberation is not the monopoly of the elite, the highly educated, or the upper castes. Anyone, regardless of gender, occupation, or social standing, who takes full shelter of the Divine, achieves the highest state of realization. The soul has no caste, class, or gender.

original_script:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा।
अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम्।।9.33।।
transliteration:
kiṁ punar brāhmaṇāḥ puṇyā bhaktā rājarṣhayas tathā
anityam asukhaṁ lokam imaṁ prāpya bhajasva mām
word_meanings: kim—how much; punaḥ—again (more); brāhmaṇāḥ—brahmanas; puṇyāḥ—righteous; bhaktāḥ—devotees; rāja-ṛiṣhayaḥ—saintly kings; tathā—also; anityam—temporary; asukham—full of miseries; lokam—world; imam—this; prāpya—having obtained; bhajasva—be engaged in loving service; mām—unto Me
translation_english: How much more this is so of the righteous brahmanas, the devotees and the saintly kings. Therefore, having come to this temporary, miserable world, engage in loving service unto Me.
deep_dive_text: The Urgency of Now. If the marginalized can attain perfection, it is obviously guaranteed for those with pious backgrounds like the saintly kings. But Krishna adds a crucial reality check: this material world is 'Anityam' (temporary) and 'Asukham' (joyless/miserable). Stop trying to build a permanent paradise in a temporary hotel. Use this rare human life to connect with the Eternal.

original_script:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः।।9.34।।
transliteration:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru
mām evaiṣhyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ
word_meanings: mat-manāḥ—always thinking of Me; bhava—become; mat—My; bhaktaḥ—devotee; mat—My; yājī—worshiper; mām—unto Me; namaskuru—offer obeisances; mām—unto Me; eva—completely; eṣhyasi—you will come; yuktvā—being absorbed; evam—thus; ātmānam—your soul; mat-parāyaṇaḥ—devoted to Me
translation_english: Engage your mind always in thinking of Me, become My devotee, offer obeisances to Me and worship Me. Being completely absorbed in Me, surely you will come to Me.
deep_dive_text: The Final Formula. Chapter 9 concludes with the simplest, yet most profound instruction. It is the core formula of Bhakti Yoga: think of the Divine, become devoted, worship, and bow down. By aligning your mind, emotions, actions, and ego towards the Supreme, your life becomes a continuous meditation, guaranteeing the ultimate union. This concludes the Ninth Chapter.

original_script: राजविद्याराजगुह्ययोगः
transliteration: Rāja Vidyā Rāja Guhya Yogaḥ
translation_english: Chapter 9: The Yoga of the Sovereign Science and Sovereign Secret
deep_dive_text: Chapter 9 reveals the most confidential knowledge of the Gita. Krishna explains that the entire universe is pervaded by His unmanifested form. All beings rest in Him, yet He is completely independent of them. He assures that anyone, regardless of their background or past mistakes, can attain ultimate liberation simply by offering their daily actions, no matter how small, with pure devotion. The chapter concludes with the powerful guarantee that His devotee never perishes.

original_script: अथ दशमोऽध्यायः - विभूतियोगः
transliteration: Vibhūti Yogaḥ
word_meanings: Vibhūti—Divine opulence/manifestation; Yogaḥ—the path of synthesis.
translation_english: Chapter 10 Summary: The Yoga of Divine Manifestations
deep_dive_text: This chapter provides a categorical inventory of the Divine's presence in the material world. Historically, it serves as a 'bridge' between abstract monism and concrete polytheism by identifying the 'best' of every category (e.g., the Lion among beasts, the Ganges among rivers) as a direct indicator of the Absolute. It establishes the 'Chatur-Shloki' (Verses 8-11) as the philosophical summary of the entire text.

original_script:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
भूय एव महाबाहो शृणु मे परमं वचः।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया।।10.1।।
transliteration:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
bhūya eva mahā-bāho śhṛiṇu me paramaṁ vachaḥ
yat te ’haṁ prīyamāṇāya vakṣhyāmi hita-kāmyayā
word_meanings: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Blessed Lord said; bhūyaḥ—again; eva—indeed; mahā-bāho—O mighty-armed one; śhṛiṇu—hear; me—My; paramam—supreme; vachaḥ—instruction; yat—which; te—to you; aham—I; prīyamāṇāya—because you are My dear friend; vakṣhyāmi—shall speak; hita-kāmyayā—for your ultimate benefit
translation_english: The Blessed Lord said: Listen again, O mighty-armed Arjuna. Because you are My dear friend, for your benefit I shall speak to you further, giving knowledge that is better than what I have already explained.
deep_dive_text: The Motivation of Love. Krishna speaks 'bhūya eva' (again). Why? Because Arjuna is 'prīyamāṇāya' (very dear to Him). True spiritual knowledge isn't forced; it flows naturally where there is love and a genuine desire for the student's ultimate benefit (hita-kāmyayā). The Divine becomes eager to reveal Himself when the seeker is eager to listen.

original_script:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः।।10.2।।
transliteration:
na me viduḥ sura-gaṇāḥ prabhavaṁ na maharṣhayaḥ
aham ādir hi devānāṁ maharṣhīṇāṁ cha sarvaśhaḥ
word_meanings: na—not; me—My; viduḥ—know; sura-gaṇāḥ—the hosts of gods; prabhavam—origin; na—nor; mahā-ṛiṣhayaḥ—the great sages; aham—I; ādiḥ—the source; hi—certainly; devānām—of the gods; mahā-ṛiṣhīṇām—of the great sages; cha—also; sarvaśhaḥ—in all respects
translation_english: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know My origin or opulences, for, in every respect, I am the source of the gods and the sages.
deep_dive_text: The Unknowable Origin. Even the ancient Vedic deities (sura-gaṇāḥ) and the most enlightened mystics (maharṣhayaḥ) cannot fully comprehend the Supreme. Why? Because you cannot see the source of the river if you are swimming downstream. Krishna is the 'ādi' (the absolute beginning) of all divine and human intellects. The created cannot fully decode the Creator.

original_script:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते।।10.3।।
transliteration:
yo mām ajam anādiṁ cha vetti loka-maheśhvaram
asammūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣhu sarva-pāpaiḥ pramuchyate
word_meanings: yaḥ—who; mām—Me; ajam—unborn; anādim—without beginning; cha—also; vetti—knows; loka—of the planets; mahā-īśhvaram—the supreme master; asammūḍhaḥ—undeluded; saḥ—he; martyeṣhu—among mortals; sarva-pāpaiḥ—from all sinful reactions; pramuchyate—is delivered
translation_english: He who knows Me as the unborn, as the beginningless, as the Supreme Lord of all the worlds—he only, undeluded among men, is freed from all sins.
deep_dive_text: The Unborn Sovereign. Knowing Krishna as 'ajam' (unborn) and 'anādim' (beginningless) isn't just a philosophical trivia point; it's a liberating paradigm shift. The person who truly grasps that they are anchored to the eternal Supreme Master becomes 'asammūḍhaḥ' (undeluded). They stop identifying with temporary anxieties and are instantly freed from the karmic reactions of mortal life.

original_script:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च।।10.4।।
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः।।10.5।।
transliteration:
buddhir jñānam asammohaḥ kṣhamā satyaṁ damaḥ śhamaḥ
sukhaṁ duḥkhaṁ bhavo ’bhāvo bhayaṁ chābhayam eva cha
ahiṁsā samatā tuṣhṭis tapo dānaṁ yaśho ’yaśhaḥ
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṁ matta eva pṛithag-vidhāḥ
word_meanings: buddhiḥ—intelligence; jñānam—knowledge; asammohaḥ—freedom from doubt; kṣhamā—forgiveness; satyam—truthfulness; damaḥ—control of the senses; śhamaḥ—control of the mind; sukham—happiness; duḥkham—distress; bhavaḥ—birth; abhāvaḥ—death; bhayam—fear; cha—also; abhayam—fearlessness; eva—also; cha—and; ahiṁsā—nonviolence; samatā—equilibrium; tuṣhṭiḥ—satisfaction; tapaḥ—penance; dānam—charity; yaśhaḥ—fame; ayaśhaḥ—infamy; bhavanti—come about; bhāvāḥ—qualities; bhūtānām—of living beings; mattaḥ—from Me; eva—alone; pṛithak-vidhāḥ—variously arranged
translation_english: Intelligence, knowledge, freedom from doubt and delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, control of the senses, control of the mind, happiness and distress, birth, death, fear, fearlessness, nonviolence, equanimity, satisfaction, austerity, charity, fame and infamy—all these various qualities of living beings are created by Me alone.
deep_dive_text: The Architect of Psychology. These two verses are a masterclass in cosmic psychology. From the highest spiritual virtues like nonviolence and truthfulness, to the basic human dualities like happiness and distress, fear and fame—all the diverse qualities of conscious beings originate from the Supreme. God is not just the creator of physical matter, but the ultimate source of the entire spectrum of human experience and emotion.

original_script:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः।।10.6।।
transliteration:
maharṣhayaḥ sapta pūrve chatvāro manavas tathā
mad-bhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣhāṁ loka imāḥ prajāḥ
word_meanings: mahā-ṛiṣhayaḥ—the great sages; sapta—seven; pūrve—before; chatvāraḥ—four; manavaḥ—Manus; tathā—also; mat-bhāvāḥ—born of Me; mānasāḥ—from the mind; jātāḥ—born; yeṣhām—of whom; loke—in the world; imāḥ—all this; prajāḥ—population
translation_english: The seven great sages and before them the four other great sages and the Manus [progenitors of mankind] come from Me, born from My mind, and all the living beings populating the various planets descend from them.
deep_dive_text: The Cosmic Ancestry. Krishna maps out the ancestral lineage of the universe. The seven foundational sages and the four Manus are not born biologically, but are 'mānasā jātā'—projected directly from the mind of the Supreme. All the bustling populations of the world, including us, are the distant descendants of this original, divine mental conception.

original_script:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः।।10.7।।
transliteration:
etāṁ vibhūtiṁ yogaṁ cha mama yo vetti tattvataḥ
so ’vikampena yogena yujyate nātra sanśhayaḥ
word_meanings: etām—all this; vibhūtim—opulence; yogam—mystic power; cha—and; mama—of Mine; yaḥ—anyone who; vetti—knows; tattvataḥ—in truth; saḥ—he; avikampena—unwavering; yogena—in devotional service; yujyate—is engaged; na—never; atra—here; sanśhayaḥ—doubt
translation_english: One who is factually convinced of this opulence and mystic power of Mine engages in unalloyed devotional service; of this there is no doubt.
deep_dive_text: The Unshakable Yogi. What happens when you truly understand the vastness of the Divine's opulence (Vibhuti)? You don't get paralyzed by the scale of the universe; you get deeply grounded. Knowing that everything is a manifestation of the Supreme leads to 'avikampena yogena'—unwavering, unshakable connection. You stop fighting the flow of life because you know exactly who is directing the currents.

original_script:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः।।10.8।।
transliteration:
ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṁ pravartate
iti matvā bhajante māṁ budhā bhāva-samanvitāḥ
word_meanings: aham—I; sarvasya—of all; prabhavaḥ—the source of generation; mattaḥ—from Me; sarvam—everything; pravartate—emanates; iti—thus; matvā—knowing; bhajante—become devoted; mām—unto Me; budhāḥ—the learned; bhāva-samanvitāḥ—with great attention and emotion
translation_english: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service and worship Me with all their hearts.
deep_dive_text: The Seed of Devotion. This verse marks the beginning of the famous 'Chatur-Shloki' (the four core verses of the Gita containing its essence). Krishna makes the ultimate claim: 'I am the source of all; everything operates because of Me.' When the truly intelligent (budhā) realize this, their response is not intellectual pride, but profound, heartfelt emotion (bhāva-samanvitāḥ). True wisdom inevitably matures into deep love.

original_script:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम्।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च।।10.9।।
transliteration:
mach-chittā mad-gata-prāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam
kathayantaśh cha māṁ nityaṁ tuṣhyanti cha ramanti cha
word_meanings: mat-chittāḥ—their minds fully engaged in Me; mat-gata-prāṇāḥ—their lives devoted to Me; bodhayantaḥ—enlightening; parasparam—one another; kathayantaḥ—speaking; cha—and; mām—of Me; nityam—perpetually; tuṣhyanti—become pleased; cha—also; ramanti—enjoy transcendental pleasure; cha—also
translation_english: The thoughts of My pure devotees dwell in Me, their lives are fully devoted to My service, and they derive great satisfaction and bliss from always enlightening one another and conversing about Me.
deep_dive_text: The Community of Love. How do enlightened people act? They don't isolate themselves defensively. Their minds are saturated with the Divine ('mach-chittā'), and their very life breath is dedicated to Him. But crucially, they find ultimate satisfaction and joy ('tuṣhyanti cha ramanti cha') in talking about Him with each other ('parasparam'). Spiritual growth is deeply relational, thriving best in a community of shared devotion.

original_script:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते।।10.10।।
transliteration:
teṣhāṁ satata-yuktānāṁ bhajatāṁ prīti-pūrvakam
dadāmi buddhi-yogaṁ taṁ yena mām upayānti te
word_meanings: teṣhām—to them; satata-yuktānām—always engaged; bhajatām—in devotional service; prīti-pūrvakam—in loving ecstasy; dadāmi—I give; buddhi-yogam—real intelligence; tam—that; yena—by which; mām—unto Me; upayānti—come; te—they
translation_english: To those who are constantly devoted to serving Me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to Me.
deep_dive_text: The Gift of Divine Intelligence. This is the third verse of the Chatur-Shloki. Krishna explains that true spiritual intelligence (Buddhi Yoga) cannot be 'earned' through mere academic study; it is 'given' (dadami) as a gift. When a seeker approaches the Divine with constant, loving devotion (priti-purvakam), the Divine personally illuminates their mind, providing the exact inner guidance needed to reach the ultimate goal.

original_script:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता।।10.11।।
transliteration:
teṣhām evānukampārtham aham ajñāna-jaṁ tamaḥ
nāśhayāmy ātma-bhāva-stho jñāna-dīpena bhāsvatā
word_meanings: teṣhām—for them; eva—certainly; anukampā-artham—to show special mercy; aham—I; ajñāna-jam—born of ignorance; tamaḥ—darkness; nāśhayāmi—destroy; ātma-bhāva-sthaḥ—situated within their hearts; jñāna-dīpena—with the lamp of knowledge; bhāsvatā—luminous
translation_english: To show them special mercy, I, dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge the darkness born of ignorance.
deep_dive_text: The Shining Lamp of Knowledge. This concludes the Chatur-Shloki (the four core verses). Krishna makes a breathtaking promise: if you take a step towards Him through devotion, He takes a thousand steps towards you. He acts as the inner guru, personally lighting the 'lamp of knowledge' inside your heart to dispel all the darkness of material ignorance. The path becomes effortless because the Divine does the heavy lifting.

original_script:
अर्जुन उवाच
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्।।10.12।।
transliteration:
arjuna uvācha
paraṁ brahma paraṁ dhāma pavitraṁ paramaṁ bhavān
puruṣhaṁ śhāśhvataṁ divyam ādi-devam ajaṁ vibhum
word_meanings: arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjuna said; param—supreme; brahma—truth; param—supreme; dhāma—abode; pavitram—pure; paramam—supreme; bhavān—You; puruṣham—personality; śhāśhvatam—eternal; divyam—transcendental; ādi-devam—the original Lord; ajam—unborn; vibhum—greatest
translation_english: Arjuna said: You are the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the ultimate abode, the purest, the Absolute Truth. You are the eternal, transcendental, original person, the unborn, the greatest.
deep_dive_text: Arjuna's Complete Acceptance. After hearing the Chatur-Shloki, Arjuna experiences a paradigm shift. His remaining doubts evaporate, and he breaks into spontaneous praise. He acknowledges Krishna not just as his friend and charioteer, but as 'Param Brahma' (the Supreme Truth) and 'Param Dhama' (the ultimate refuge). This is the moment the student fully realizes the magnitude of the Teacher.

original_script:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे।।10.13।।
transliteration:
āhus tvām ṛiṣhayaḥ sarve devarṣhir nāradas tathā
asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṁ chaiva bravīṣhi me
word_meanings: āhuḥ—say; tvām—of You; ṛiṣhayaḥ—sages; sarve—all; deva-ṛiṣhiḥ—the sage among the demigods; nāradaḥ—Narada; tathā—also; asitaḥ—Asita; devalaḥ—Devala; vyāsaḥ—Vyasa; svayam—personally; cha—and; eva—certainly; bravīṣhi—You are explaining; me—unto me
translation_english: All the great sages such as Narada, Asita, Devala and Vyasa confirm this truth about You, and now You Yourself are declaring it to me.
deep_dive_text: The Verification of Truth. Arjuna isn't just relying on blind faith; he cross-references Krishna's claims with the highest spiritual authorities of his time—Vyasa, Narada, Asita, and Devala. True spiritual knowledge always aligns across authentic teachers. Arjuna finds absolute comfort in the fact that the universal tradition and his personal experience are now perfectly synchronized.

original_script:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः।।10.14।।
transliteration:
sarvam etad ṛitaṁ manye yan māṁ vadasi keśhava
na hi te bhagavan vyaktiṁ vidur devā na dānavāḥ
word_meanings: sarvam—all; etat—this; ṛitam—truth; manye—I accept; yat—which; mām—unto me; vadasi—You tell; keśhava—O Krishna; na—never; hi—certainly; te—Your; bhagavan—O Supreme Lord; vyaktim—revelation; viduḥ—can know; devāḥ—the demigods; na—nor; dānavāḥ—the demons
translation_english: O Krishna, I totally accept as truth all that You have told me. Neither the demigods nor the demons, O Lord, can understand Your personality.
deep_dive_text: Beyond Good and Evil Intellects. Arjuna admits that the Divine personality is beyond the comprehension of both 'Devas' (beings of light/goodness) and 'Danavas' (beings of darkness/ego). God cannot be decoded by mere intellect or power. The Absolute Truth can only be known when the Absolute Truth chooses to reveal itself.

original_script:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते।।10.15।।
transliteration:
svayam evātmanātmānaṁ vettha tvaṁ puruṣhottama
bhūta-bhāvana bhūteśha deva-deva jagat-pate
word_meanings: svayam—personally; eva—certainly; ātmanā—by Yourself; ātmānam—Yourself; vettha—know; tvam—You; puruṣha-uttama—O Supreme Person; bhūta-bhāvana—O origin of everything; bhūta-īśha—O Lord of everything; deva-deva—O Lord of all demigods; jagat-pate—O Lord of the entire universe
translation_english: Indeed, You alone know Yourself by Your own internal potency, O Supreme Person, origin of all, Lord of all beings, God of gods, Lord of the universe!
deep_dive_text: The Unknowable Knower. If neither gods nor demons can understand the Supreme, who can? Arjuna concludes that only God can truly know God. He stacks magnificent titles—Bhuta-bhavana (Creator), Jagat-pate (Master of the universe)—to express his awe. The infinite can only be measured by the infinite.

original_script:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि।।10.16।।
transliteration:
vaktum arhasy aśheṣheṇa divyā hy ātma-vibhūtayaḥ
yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāns tvaṁ vyāpya tiṣhṭhasi
word_meanings: vaktum—to tell; arhasi—You should; aśheṣheṇa—in detail; divyāḥ—divine; hi—certainly; ātma—Your own; vibhūtayaḥ—opulences; yābhiḥ—by which; vibhūtibhiḥ—opulences; lokān—all the worlds; imān—these; tvam—You; vyāpya—pervading; tiṣhṭhasi—remain
translation_english: Please tell me in detail of Your divine opulences by which You pervade all these worlds.
deep_dive_text: The Request for the Map. Arjuna shifts from awe to practical application. He wants to know how to 'see' the Divine in the everyday world. He asks Krishna to describe His 'Vibhutis'—the divine manifestations or opulences—so he can recognize the presence of the infinite while operating within the finite world.

original_script:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया।।10.17।।
transliteration:
kathaṁ vidyām ahaṁ yogins tvāṁ sadā parichintayan
keṣhu keṣhu cha bhāveṣhu chintyo ’si bhagavan mayā
word_meanings: katham—how; vidyām aham—shall I know; yogin—O supreme mystic; tvām—You; sadā—always; parichintayan—thinking of; keṣhu—in what; keṣhu—in what; cha—also; bhāveṣhu—natures; chintyaḥ asi—You are to be remembered; bhagavan—O Supreme; mayā—by me
translation_english: O Krishna, O supreme mystic, how shall I constantly think of You, and how shall I know You? In what various forms are You to be remembered, O Supreme Personality of Godhead?
deep_dive_text: The Art of Constant Remembrance. Arjuna is asking for meditation targets. It’s hard to focus on an invisible, formless Absolute. Arjuna asks, 'When I look at the world, what should I look at to remind me of You?' This is the foundation of 'Vibhuti Yoga'—using the awe-inspiring aspects of nature to trigger spiritual mindfulness.

original_script:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि शृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्।।10.18।।
transliteration:
vistareṇātmano yogaṁ vibhūtiṁ cha janārdana
bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛiptir hi śhṛiṇvato nāsti me ’mṛitam
word_meanings: vistareṇa—in detail; ātmanaḥ—Your; yogam—mystic power; vibhūtim—opulences; cha—also; janārdana—O killer of the atheists; bhūyaḥ—again; kathaya—describe; tṛiptiḥ—satisfaction; hi—certainly; śhṛiṇvataḥ—hearing; na asti—there is not; me—my; amṛitam—nectar
translation_english: O Janardana, again please describe in detail the mystic power of Your opulences. I am never satiated in hearing about You, for the more I hear the more I want to taste the nectar of Your words.
deep_dive_text: The Nectar of Divine Discourse. Arjuna's spiritual appetite has been fully awakened. He compares Krishna's words to 'Amritam' (immortal nectar). Unlike material pleasures which cause fatigue or boredom over time, spiritual discussions only increase the thirst for more. He is eagerly inviting Krishna to expand on the glory of the universe.

original_script:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे।।10.19।।
transliteration:
śhrī bhagavān uvācha
hanta te kathayiṣhyāmi divyā hy ātma-vibhūtayaḥ
prādhānyataḥ kuru-śhreṣhṭha nāsty anto vistarasya me
word_meanings: śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said; hanta—yes; te—unto you; kathayiṣhyāmi—I shall speak; divyāḥ—divine; hi—certainly; ātma-vibhūtayaḥ—personal opulences; prādhānyataḥ—which are principal; kuru-śhreṣhṭha—O best of the Kurus; na asti—there is not; antaḥ—limit; vistarasya—to the extent; me—My
translation_english: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Yes, I will tell you of My splendorous manifestations, but only of those which are prominent, O best of the Kurus, for My opulence is limitless.
deep_dive_text: The Infinite Catalog. Krishna agrees to answer, but with a caveat: His manifestations are absolutely endless. Trying to list them all is like trying to count the grains of sand on Earth. Therefore, He will only provide the 'Pradhanyatah' (the most prominent or principal ones) as a representative sample of His infinite glory. The exhibition of divine opulence is about to begin.

original_script:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च।।10.20।।
transliteration:
aham ātmā guḍākeśha sarva-bhūtāśhaya-sthitaḥ
aham ādiśh cha madhyaṁ cha bhūtānām anta eva cha
word_meanings: aham—I; ātmā—the soul; guḍākeśha—O Arjuna (conqueror of sleep); sarva-bhūta—of all living entities; āśhaya-sthitaḥ—situated within the heart; aham—I; ādiḥ—the origin; cha—also; madhyam—middle; cha—also; bhūtānām—of all living entities; antaḥ—end; eva—certainly; cha—and
translation_english: I am the Supersoul, O Arjuna, seated in the hearts of all living entities. I am the beginning, the middle and the end of all beings.
deep_dive_text: The Core of the Cosmos. Before listing His external opulences, Krishna establishes the ultimate internal truth. He is the 'Atma' inside every single being. He is the origin (birth), the middle (life), and the end (death). He places Himself directly at the center of Arjuna's own heart, stating that before looking for God in the spectacular stars, one must first recognize Him within.

original_script:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी।।10.21।।
transliteration:
ādityānām ahaṁ viṣhṇur jyotiṣhāṁ ravir aṁśhumān
marīchir marutām asmi nakṣhatrāṇām ahaṁ śhaśhī
word_meanings: ādityānām—of the Adityas; aham—I; viṣhṇuḥ—the Supreme Lord; jyotiṣhām—of all luminaries; raviḥ—the sun; aṁśhu-mān—radiant; marīchiḥ—Marichi; marutām—of the Marutis; asmi—I am; nakṣhatrāṇām—of the stars; aham—I; śhaśhī—the moon
translation_english: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu, of lights I am the radiant sun, of the Maruts I am Marichi, and among the stars I am the moon.
deep_dive_text: The Light of the World. Krishna begins his cosmic catalog by identifying with the most powerful sources of light and energy known to humanity. The Sun is the sustainer of life on Earth, and the Moon is the soothing light of the night sky. He is telling Arjuna to look up: the brilliant radiance of the universe is just a fraction of His divine glow.

original_script:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना।।10.22।।
transliteration:
vedānāṁ sāma-vedo ’smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ
indriyāṇāṁ manaśh chāsmi bhūtānām asmi chetanā
word_meanings: vedānām—of all the Vedas; sāma-vedaḥ—the Sama Veda; asmi—I am; devānām—of all the demigods; asmi—I am; vāsavaḥ—the heavenly king, Indra; indriyāṇām—of all the senses; manaḥ—the mind; cha—also; asmi—I am; bhūtānām—in all living beings; asmi—I am; chetanā—the living force (consciousness)
translation_english: Of the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; of the demigods I am Indra, the king of heaven; of the senses I am the mind; and in living beings I am the living force [consciousness].
deep_dive_text: The Essence of Experience. Krishna is not just out there in the heavens; He is the mechanics of our internal world. He is the 'Mana' (mind) among the senses, the central processor of human experience. Even more profoundly, He is 'Chetana' (consciousness itself). Without Him, we are just biology; with Him, we are awake and aware.

original_script:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्।।10.23।।
transliteration:
rudrāṇāṁ śhaṅkaraśh chāsmi vitteśho yakṣha-rakṣhasām
vasūnāṁ pāvakaśh chāsmi meruḥ śhikhariṇām aham
word_meanings: rudrāṇām—of all the Rudras; śhaṅkaraḥ—Lord Shiva; cha—also; asmi—I am; vitta-īśhaḥ—the lord of wealth (Kuvera); yakṣha-rakṣhasām—of the Yakshas and Rakshasas; vasūnām—of the Vasus; pāvakaḥ—fire; cha—also; asmi—I am; meruḥ—Meru; śhikhariṇām—of all mountains; aham—I
translation_english: Of all the Rudras I am Lord Shiva, of the Yakshas and Rakshasas I am the Lord of wealth [Kuvera], of the Vasus I am fire [Agni], and of mountains I am Meru.
deep_dive_text: The Peaks of Power. Here, Krishna aligns Himself with the most formidable forces of transformation and stability. Lord Shiva (Shankara) represents the ultimate dissolving force that clears the way for the new. Fire (Pavaka) purifies everything it touches, and Mount Meru stands as the unshakable, mythical axis of the universe. He is both the destructive fire and the unmoving mountain.

original_script:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः।।10.24।।
transliteration:
purodhasāṁ cha mukhyaṁ māṁ viddhi pārtha bṛihaspatim
senānīnām ahaṁ skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ
word_meanings: purodhasām—of all priests; cha—also; mukhyam—the chief; mām—Me; viddhi—understand; pārtha—O son of Pritha; bṛihaspatim—Brihaspati; senānīnām—of all commanders; aham—I; skandaḥ—Kartikeya; sarasām—of all reservoirs of water; asmi—I am; sāgaraḥ—the ocean
translation_english: Of priests, O Arjuna, know Me to be the chief, Brihaspati. Of generals I am Kartikeya, and of bodies of water I am the ocean.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Authority. Brihaspati is the guru of the gods, representing ultimate spiritual wisdom, while Skanda (Kartikeya) is the fierce commander of the celestial armies, representing ultimate martial power. And among waters, He is the vast, unfathomable ocean. Krishna claims the peak of intellect, the peak of strength, and the peak of depth.

original_script:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः।।10.25।।
transliteration:
maharṣhīṇāṁ bhṛigur ahaṁ girām asmy ekam akṣharam
yajñānāṁ japa-yajño ’smi sthāvarāṇāṁ himālayaḥ
word_meanings: mahā-ṛiṣhīṇām—among the great sages; bhṛiguḥ—Bhrigu; aham—I; girām—of vibrations; asmi—I am; ekam akṣharam—the syllable Om; yajñānām—of sacrifices; japa-yajñaḥ—chanting; asmi—I am; sthāvarāṇām—of immovable things; himālayaḥ—the Himalayan mountains
translation_english: Of the great sages I am Bhrigu; of vibrations I am the transcendental Om. Of sacrifices I am the chanting of the holy names [japa], and of immovable things I am the Himalayas.
deep_dive_text: The Sacred Sound and Silence. Krishna identifies with 'Om,' the primal sound vibrating through creation. Notably, among all complex Vedic rituals (yajnas), He says He is 'Japa'—the simple, quiet, repetitive chanting of holy names. It highlights that the most powerful spiritual practice isn't loud or expensive, but deeply internal. He is the quiet strength of the Himalayas and the subtle vibration of a chanted mantra.

original_script:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः।।10.26।।
transliteration:
aśhvatthaḥ sarva-vṛikṣhāṇāṁ devarṣhīṇāṁ cha nāradaḥ
gandharvāṇāṁ chitrarathaḥ siddhānāṁ kapilo muniḥ
word_meanings: aśhvatthaḥ—the banyan tree; sarva-vṛikṣhāṇām—of all trees; deva-ṛiṣhīṇām—of all the sages among the demigods; cha—and; nāradaḥ—Narada; gandharvāṇām—of the citizens of the Gandharva planet; chitrarathaḥ—Chitraratha; siddhānām—of all those who are perfected; kapilaḥ muniḥ—Kapila Muni
translation_english: Of all trees I am the banyan tree, and of the sages among the demigods I am Narada. Of the Gandharvas I am Chitraratha, and among perfected beings I am the sage Kapila.
deep_dive_text: The Roots of Perfection. The Ashvattha (Banyan/Peepal) tree is a symbol of endurance and deeply rooted spirituality. Narada is the cosmic musician and ultimate messenger of devotion. By identifying with these figures, Krishna shows that He is the majestic beauty in nature, the wandering joy of the divine singer, and the intellectual perfection of the great philosopher Kapila.

original_script:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्।
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्।।10.27।।
transliteration:
uchchaiḥśhravasam aśhvānāṁ viddhi mām amṛitodbhavam
airāvataṁ gajendrāṇāṁ narāṇāṁ cha narādhipam
word_meanings: uchchaiḥśhravasam—Uchchaihshrava; aśhvānām—among horses; viddhi—know; mām—Me; amṛita-udbhavam—produced from the churning of the ocean; airāvatam—Airavata; gaja-indrāṇām—of lordly elephants; narāṇām—among human beings; cha—and; nara-adhipam—the king
translation_english: Of horses know Me to be Uchchaihshrava, produced during the churning of the ocean for nectar. Of lordly elephants I am Airavata, and among men I am the monarch.
deep_dive_text: The Churning of Greatness. Uchchaihshrava (the flying horse) and Airavata (the multi-trunked elephant) emerged from the mythic 'Samudra Manthan' (churning of the cosmic ocean). They represent the supreme treasures that arise from immense struggle and purification. Furthermore, Krishna states He is the 'monarch' among humans—the organizing principle that brings order and stability to society.

original_script:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्।
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः।।10.28।।
transliteration:
āyudhānām ahaṁ vajraṁ dhenūnām asmi kāmadhuk
prajanaśh chāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ
word_meanings: āyudhānām—of all weapons; aham—I; vajram—the thunderbolt; dhenūnām—of cows; asmi—I am; kāma-dhuk—the surabhi cow; prajanaḥ—the cause for begetting children; cha—and; asmi—I am; kandarpaḥ—Cupid; sarpāṇām—of serpents; asmi—I am; vāsukiḥ—Vasuki
translation_english: Of weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows I am the surabhi. Of causes for procreation I am Kandarpa, the god of love, and of serpents I am Vasuki.
deep_dive_text: The Power of Desire and Creation. Krishna claims to be the Thunderbolt (Vajra)—the most unstoppable weapon. Yet, He is also the Surabhi cow, the gentle provider of unlimited nourishment. Surprisingly, He identifies with 'Kandarpa' (Cupid), stating that the desire for procreation—when aligned with the continuation of life and dharma—is a sacred, divine expression, not something to be shunned.

original_script:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्।
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्।।10.29।।
transliteration:
anantaśh chāsmi nāgānāṁ varuṇo yādasām aham
pitṝīṇām aryamā chāsmi yamaḥ sanyamatām aham
word_meanings: anantaḥ—Ananta; cha—also; asmi—I am; nāgānām—of the many-hooded serpents; varuṇaḥ—the demigod controlling the water; yādasām—of all aquatics; aham—I; pitṝīṇām—of the ancestors; aryamā—Aryama; cha—also; asmi—I am; yamaḥ—the controller of death; sanyamatām—of all regulators; aham—I
translation_english: Of the many-hooded Nagas I am Ananta, and among the aquatics I am the demigod Varuna. Of departed ancestors I am Aryama, and among the dispensers of law I am Yama, the lord of death.
deep_dive_text: The Ultimate Regulator. Ananta is the infinite cosmic serpent holding the universe, representing the boundless fabric of space and time. But the most striking claim here is, 'I am Yama, the lord of death.' Death is often feared, but Krishna presents it as 'sanyamatam' (the ultimate regulator). Death is the divine mechanism that enforces the laws of karma and keeps the cosmic cycle moving gracefully.

original_script:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम्।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्।।10.30।।
transliteration:
prahlādaśh chāsmi daityānāṁ kālaḥ kalayatām aham
mṛigāṇāṁ cha mṛigendro ’haṁ vainateyaśh cha pakṣhiṇām
word_meanings: prahlādaḥ—Prahlada; cha—also; asmi—I am; daityānām—of the demons; kālaḥ—time; kalayatām—of subduers; aham—I; mṛigāṇām—of animals; cha—and; mṛiga-indraḥ—the lion; aham—I; vainateyaḥ—Garuda; cha—also; pakṣhiṇām—of birds
translation_english: Among the Daitya demons I am the devoted Prahlada, among subduers I am time, among beasts I am the lion, and among birds I am Garuda.
deep_dive_text: The Devotion Within the Demonic. Prahlada was born into a family of atheistic demons (Daityas) but became the greatest devotee. Krishna shows that divine love can bloom in the darkest, most hostile environments. He also identifies as 'Kala' (Time)—the ultimate subduer that eventually wears down every physical structure and ego. He is the majestic power of the lion and the swift, soaring devotion of Garuda.

original_script:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी।।10.31।।
transliteration:
pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śhastra-bhṛitām aham
jhaṣhāṇāṁ makaraśh chāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī
word_meanings: pavanaḥ—the wind; pavatām—of all that purifies; asmi—I am; rāmaḥ—Rama; śhastra-bhṛitām—of the wielders of weapons; aham—I; jhaṣhāṇām—of all fishes; makaraḥ—the shark; cha—also; asmi—I am; srotasām—of flowing rivers; asmi—I am; jāhnavī—the River Ganges
translation_english: Of purifiers I am the wind, of the wielders of weapons I am Rama, of fishes I am the shark, and of flowing rivers I am the Ganges.
deep_dive_text: The Supreme Purifier. The wind sweeps away staleness, and the Ganges (Jahnavi) washes away spiritual impurities. Krishna identifies with Rama, the ultimate warrior of righteousness who wields his weapon not for conquest, but for dharma. Even beneath the ocean's surface, He is the apex predator, the Makara (shark/crocodile), representing sheer, unchallengeable dominance in the aquatic realm.

original_script:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्।।10.32।।
transliteration:
sargāṇām ādir antaśh cha madhyaṁ chaivāham arjuna
adhyātma-vidyā vidyānāṁ vādaḥ pravadatām aham
word_meanings: sargāṇām—of all creations; ādiḥ—the beginning; antaḥ—end; cha—and; madhyam—middle; cha—also; eva—certainly; aham—I am; arjuna—O Arjuna; adhyātma-vidyā—spiritual knowledge; vidyānām—of all education; vādaḥ—the natural conclusion; pravadatām—of arguments; aham—I am
translation_english: Of all creations I am the beginning and the end and also the middle, O Arjuna. Of all sciences I am the spiritual science of the self, and among logicians I am the conclusive truth.
deep_dive_text: The Science of the Self. Krishna bookends the universe again—He is the start, middle, and end of everything. More practically, He claims that among all fields of education (Vidyas), He is 'Adhyatma-vidya' (the science of the soul). Without knowing the self, all other knowledge is just data. In debates and arguments, He is not the squabbling or the rhetoric; He is 'Vada'—the final, irrefutable, conclusive truth.

original_script:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः।।10.33।।
transliteration:
akṣharāṇām akāro ’smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya cha
aham evākṣhayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṁ viśhvato-mukhaḥ
word_meanings: akṣharāṇām—of letters; akāraḥ—the first letter; asmi—I am; dvandvaḥ—the dual; sāmāsikasya—of compounds; cha—and; aham—I am; eva—certainly; akṣhayaḥ—inexhaustible; kālaḥ—time; dhātā—the creator; aham—I am; viśhvataḥ-mukhaḥ—Brahma
translation_english: Of letters I am the letter A, and among compound words I am the dual compound. I am also inexhaustible time, and of creators I am Brahma.
deep_dive_text: The Foundation of Communication. The letter 'A' (Akara) is the foundational sound produced when the mouth opens; all other sounds are modifications of it. Thus, Krishna is the very basis of language and communication. He re-emphasizes that He is 'Akshaya Kala' (endless time). While physical time subdues beings (as in verse 30), inexhaustible time is the eternal canvas on which the creator (Brahma) paints the cosmos.

original_script:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा।।10.34।।
transliteration:
mṛityuḥ sarva-haraśh chāham udbhavaśh cha bhaviṣhyatām
kīrtiḥ śhrīr vāk cha nārīṇāṁ smṛitir medhā dhṛitiḥ kṣhamā
word_meanings: mṛityuḥ—death; sarva-haraḥ—all-devouring; cha—and; aham—I am; udbhavaḥ—generating principle; cha—also; bhaviṣhyatām—of future manifestations; kīrtiḥ—fame; śhrīḥ—opulence or beauty; vāk—fine speech; cha—and; nārīṇām—of women; smṛitiḥ—memory; medhā—intelligence; dhṛitiḥ—firmness; kṣhamā—patience
translation_english: I am all-devouring death, and I am the generating principle of all that is yet to be. Among women I am fame, fortune, fine speech, memory, intelligence, steadfastness and patience.
deep_dive_text: The Great Reset and The Seven Graces.
